prompt:
A hyper-realistic architectural photograph of a long, narrow medieval stone corridor stretching deep into the frame using extreme linear perspective. The corridor is shot from ground level at one end, looking straight down its length toward the far wall. The floor is ancient worn limestone, the walls are rough-hewn dark grey granite blocks, and the vaulted ceiling is low and oppressive.
At the very far end of the corridor — roughly 40 meters away — a single ornate wrought-iron candelabra mounted on the wall burns with intense warm golden-amber flame, the only source of light in the entire scene. This far end glows brilliantly with rich warm orange and gold tones.
Suspended from the ceiling at perfectly equal intervals along the entire length of the corridor are exactly seven thin rectangular panels of frosted semi-transparent glass, each one hanging perpendicular to the corridor's length like a series of doors left ajar. The panels are identical in size and material — each roughly two meters tall and one meter wide, with thin iron frames.
The critical visual effect: each successive panel from the far end toward the camera is dramatically darker and less luminous than the one before it, following a steep exponential decay. The first panel (closest to the candelabra) glows with rich warm amber light, clearly backlit. The second panel glows noticeably dimmer. The third dimmer still. By the fourth and fifth panels the warm color has almost entirely faded to cold grey-blue shadow. The sixth panel is barely a faint grey outline. The seventh and final panel — closest to the camera — is nearly indistinguishable from the dark corridor walls, receiving almost zero light, existing as little more than a dark translucent silhouette in near-total blackness.
The cumulative dimming effect should feel mathematically precise — like each panel transmits only 30% of the light it receives and absorbs the rest. The overall mood is cold, silent, and austere. The only warmth in the entire image exists at the far end. The near end where the viewer stands is in almost complete darkness.
Photorealistic, long-exposure architectural photography style, shot on a medium format camera with a 35mm lens, f/8 aperture, high dynamic range. No people, no text, no labels, no diagrams, no symbols, no numbers, no annotations, no floating elements of any kind.  Negative Prompt:
people, human figures, text, labels, numbers, equations, diagrams, graphs, annotations, arrows, glowing particles, neon, sci-fi elements, modern architecture, bright overall lighting, flat lighting, uniform brightness, mirrors, windows, sunlight
metaphor: Seven frosted glass panels absorb a fixed fraction of light each; cumulative exponential decay means the panel nearest the viewer — like early network layers during backprop — receives almost no signal at all.
